title: Mellow Ethiopian Squash Tagine
description: A romantic Ethiopian dish featuring roasted squash, citrusy flavors, and a hint of spice, all slow-cooked to perfection in a ceramic tagine.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the squash cubes with 2 tablespoons of olive oil, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the squash cubes in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 30 minutes, or until they are tender and lightly browned.
In a large ceramic tagine, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the tagine and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the tagine and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Stir in the ground cumin and coriander and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the roasted squash, shio-koji mixture, and lemon juice to the tagine, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together and the squash is very tender.
